Alright, welcome to the next lesson in the course, Custom Fitness Nutrition and Accountability. So if you want to create something that is completely custom for your challengers or for your members, this is exactly how you'll do it. So we're calling it the Blueprint. This Blueprint is a document that you'll fill out with your challengers or clients that walks them through each one of these areas that is specific to them. So this is perfect for online clients, so typically one-on-one online clients. It's great for HTTP clients where customizable nutrition, fitness and accountability come with their program. And it can be used for six-week or 42-day challengers, but we don't recommend it. We typically recommend them sticking to the template in nutrition. It is much more scalable and it is easier to understand without a ton of explanation and it's easier for your staff to understand. So it can be used for them, but we would recommend sticking to the template and stuff. This will be completed on WAN day, so you'll need to set aside 30 minutes for a one-on-one consultation or orientation. So because it's completely customizable to the person, you can't really do these in smaller large groups. They do need to be one-on-one, so make sure that per challenger that you have to meet with on WAN day, you set aside 30 minutes to go through this Blueprint document with them. That being said, let's take a look at the Blueprint itself. We're going to do an overarching overview of the Blueprint and then in following videos, we're going to get more granular to look at the specific fitness, nutrition and accountability that it talks about or references. So let's jump right into it now. What we'll see is this is the beginning of the document. You'll need to put your logo here. And so right underneath the video that you're watching now, there's going to be a button that you can click on and you can download this Blueprint for yourself. When you do that, you'll switch this logo out with your own logo and then you'll switch it out again here. So you're actually printing these off and you are going to be handwriting them with your clients. This isn't something you do on your computer. It prevents much more value when you're sitting down and talking with them and writing everything down. So that's why we have it set up like this. And then when you're finished with it, you'll make a copy of it. You'll give them a copy of it and you'll keep a copy of it. That way you guys both have an understanding of exactly what was talked about and you can always reference it. So put your logo here, put your logo here. The first thing that we're going to jump into is goal setting. So we want to get a good idea of their expectations and set expectations appropriately. So basically what it goes over is what are your long-term goals and then us as trainers are going to let them know if those are realistic goals, if those are expectations that we can meet. And if not, we find a compromise as to what you truly believe in as a gym owner or fitness coach. They're able to achieve and in addition to what they really want to achieve, you guys will talk together about those goals and set some great long-term goals. Then you'll get an understanding of when they'd like to reach them by. Again, they might say, I want to lose 30 pounds by next week and you as a coach say, well, basically this is what would happen or this is how long it would take to lose 30 pounds. Does that sound reasonable? Does that sound doable? So right off the bat, you are curbing their expectations to what we typically know is doable. And then finally, you'll want to ask, where do you feel like you struggle the most? This is great for a coach to know because this is a chance for them to be honest with you and they'll say like, I'm horrible with nutrition or I love sweets or I drink a lot of alcohol or whatever it may be, this is their chance to tell you where you have your work cut out for you. And so you write that down in as much detail as you can. So that is typically where you would start unless you start off with the weigh-in, which is what I like to do, is start off with the weigh-in and then you'll put the stats here. So you'll put their name, age, height, weight, and then their body fat percentage that you've got from the first weigh-in before they met with you. That cuts the time down a little bit. If you have like a front desk or somebody else that's doing all the weigh-ins with the person and then maybe they just come to your office once that part has been done, it'll cut out like five, 10 minutes of time. So then you fill this out with their unique stats. The next part after you've assessed goals and talked with them about their goals is you're going to be going over the phases of transformation, right? Fairly simple, but again, it sets expectations for them so they know what to expect as they begin their program with you. So the first one we call the foundation phase. This is when they are beginning to exercise. They're changing eating habits. They begin tracking calories. They begin tracking macros. We're optimizing their sleep. We're increasing their water intake and we are beginning their supplement program, right? So that is what's happening in the foundation phase. Once we have that completed, once all of that stuff has been going for a week or two, then they're in the results phase, right? And that's where they're going to see their rapid weight loss, their lean muscle gain, their great form, body fat reduction, improved flexibility, energy, boosting confidence. These are things that you want to tell them like, hey, once you hit the results phase, which can be a week after or two weeks after the foundations phase, like this is when all the things that you're hoping for come. Then finally, we have the transform phase. That's when they're finally transformed and they now have new health conscious lifestyle. They maintain results with less external accountability. So a lot of the time people say, well, I don't know if I want to train her for the whole rest of my life. This is a great way to kind of explain to them that like, look, you might not need me forever. If you can really optimize your learning here and apply this stuff and make it happen, which we know if they find value in accountability and your workouts, they will be with you for a very long time. But it's good to let them know that your goal as a trainer isn't to keep them with you forever. You want to teach them. You want to teach them how they can have a healthy lifestyle without as much external accountability and more of it will be internal accountability. They're holding themselves accountable. That's a goal of you and your gym. And then they can continue with a less aggressive program. So maybe you sign somebody up for a 12 week HTTP and they're training with you three days a week, whatever it may be. You can say, look, once we get to this maintenance phase, once we get to this transform phase and you have a lot of it on your own or the accountability is on your own, then you are able to maintain without as much strenuous aggressive workouts that we're putting you through. It's just a great way to show them that you are really looking out for them. So again, we'll slide down and then we have you basically writing down what their long term goals are from that you captured from above. And then the approximate time that we are going to take to reach them. Just to overview again. Then we have their six week goals. If you're running a 12 week HTTP program and you're using this, you can easily just cross this off and write 12 week. But this gives us an idea of what we're trying to accomplish in six weeks, whether it's 20 pounds or 25 pounds or 5% body fat. That's where you would write this goal here. So then you kind of break it down to them on what they need to accomplish on a weekly basis and that really puts it in perspective for them. And they'll say, okay, I need to lose three pounds a week or I need to lose, you know, three and a half pounds a week. And then the rest of this plan goes into exactly how we're going to do that. So now we have identified our weekly goals. So we are going to move into the after you discuss the goals and you write that down, we're moving right into the nutrition piece of this. So this, we are going to be using the Harris-Benedick equation and this is for counting calories. This is a way to find the BMR. And once you have the BMR, you can manipulate it to reduce the amount of calories they need to maintain their weight and put them into a deficit. So we will go over this specifically in the custom nutrition portion of it. But to give you an overview, we are going to be tracking calories and right here it recommends that they use my fitness pal to track it or something like that, whatever app they prefer. There's a lot of apps out there now that track calories. But the point is that we want them to use one of those apps. We reference my fitness pal because it's super popular. They can screenshot that to you at the end of each day or week, probably week. So you don't have so many screenshots and you can look at it and see exactly what they've been eating. So we want to make sure that we give them a specific calorie goal. And that's what we're going to do with this formula. There's one for women up here and the bottom one is for men. And by the time you input the numbers into it, you will have how many calories they should be eating per day. So the next thing that we're going to do is once we have the amount of calories they should be eating per day, we're going to break it up between three meals and a couple snacks. And just like our templated nutrition, it has breakfast, lunch, dinner, and a couple snacks in between. So you are going to then break up for them how many calories you'd like them to have for each one of those meals and snacks. So like as an example, let's say their total calorie goal would be 1500 calories. Maybe you do 300 for breakfast, 300 for lunch, 300 for... I'm sorry, that wouldn't work. 400, 400, 400 breakfast, lunch, dinner, that puts them at 1200 and then 150 for each snack. As an example, you'll break it up however you see best fit once you know what their total calorie goal is. So phase one for nutrition is just have them start counting calories using an app, preferably like My Fitness Pal, and sending you screenshots on a weekly basis of their weekly goals and their weekly results. And then phase two would be breaking it up by meals so they're not having all 1500 calories for dinner. And then phase three, as you can see, we're getting into macros. So you can assign whatever macros you want to them, but we have it set up for 40% protein, 40% carbs, 20% fat. Just as a pre-built whatever, you can cross it off and write what you want in there. But this one typically works, this split works pretty well for weight loss. And again, on My Fitness Pal or a lot of the other apps, you can just click a tab and it says macros and it will say exactly what their macros are. So your goal is to tell them and explain to them to stick between certain macros. So this is totally customizable based on what you feel makes sense as a coach and then have them stick to those guidelines. So that covers the customizable nutrition piece. We'll go into more details when we get to nutrition. But then the next part of the blueprint is the workout plan. So they'll be getting to, the goal is to burn 3500 calories, which is one pound of fat. The goal is to have them burn that each week as well to get the second half of their results. The majority of the results as we know are going to be coming from nutrition. But we want to set a really good workout plan for them as well. So they're burning a ton of calories each week. So when you get to the workout portion of the plan, you can see that we have listed how many calories just some regular activity would typically burn. So light cardio, which would be like walking or a recumbent bike or something real slow, about 115 calories every 30 minutes or so. Moderate cardio like jogging, elliptical, stationary bike, 300 calories every 30 minutes or so. And then it has group classes, high intensity group classes. And then you'll see it says our proprietary at home and engine workouts. So if they're an online client of yours, or they're a client of yours at the gym, whether it's HTTP or however you're training them, we show a 400 calorie per 30 minute burn, which is higher than the other ones, which is why they should train with you. And then finally, the bottom one, this is for ascension to a higher level says our HTTP semi private training. And this is 800 calories. We don't annotate it, but the 800 calories is based on an hour. So it's kind of the same as the other ones. But it's typically HTTP training is an hour long session. So we're just doubling your normal workout, which would be 30 minutes. But if they can only make it in twice a week, maybe that's why they choose this one or whatever. So once we identify how many calories they're going to be burning or based on their goals, we're going to be able to write that down in a workout template Monday through Sunday. So we're going to write down how many workouts they have and how and it's going to be based on the on the goal that they set for losing weight. So if you gave them a goal of burning 3500 calories per week, if that's what you assigned to them, then you need to write down 3500 calories worth of workouts in this chart below. So maybe Monday, Wednesday, Friday, they have HTTP workouts with you. 800 calories, that's 2400 total. Right. So what do they need to do Tuesday, Thursday, Saturday and Sunday to make up the difference? They still have to burn and another 1100 calories. Right. So maybe you plug in some jogging. Do you like to jog? Yeah. Okay. Jog for a half hour on Tuesday, Thursday. Okay. So we know that's going to give them 600 calories, 500 calories left. Right. Say, hey, why don't you do one of our at home workouts on Saturday and Sunday will be your off day or take Saturday and Sunday off and instead on Tuesday, Thursday, do a jog and on Thursday, add in one of our at home workouts as soon as you get home from your jog. Right. However, you want to break it up, but try to get them as many calories burned as possible. That's going to be doing a lot of work for you. If the more you can get them to execute outside of your workouts, the better it's going to be for their results. Right. So that's where you write it in here. That's where you plug in their off days. So you can just write the words off on the days that they have off, but otherwise you're writing a total amount of calories. So in the blue area, you're writing what their workouts going to be. And then in the white area underneath, how many calories that's going to burn. Right. And then so then underneath, as you scroll down, it'll say total calories burned per week. That's where you total everything up and we write down how many calories they're burning from workouts. Right. They're already going to be burning a ton of calories, just being alive and walking around and maybe they have a Fitbit or something tracking it. But this is calories from working out in addition to being alive. Right. And then we're going to write down specific areas that they want to focus on. Right. This is more for your clients than it is for like you to know, because you know that if they want to tone their arms, well, you know, fat's going to burn, uh, typically evenly throughout the body and you're not going to spot reduce just their arms. Um, but it's good for them to know that you're building something customized. So maybe in some of the workouts that you recommend to them, it has some more biceps or triceps or, uh, different exercises catered more towards what they told you they want to achieve. You can easily build muscle in specific areas. So you can maybe include more muscle building exercises in the areas they want to build. Right. And then ideal days off. This is for you to take in consideration when they would like to have off just based on their, their normal, uh, life, you know, based on what they have that they do every single day. Right. So, um, this is for you to write like Saturday and Sunday or whatever. Um, so then you guys both understand like when their off day is. So underneath here, you're going to say, you're going to, uh, see that it says we're going to send you the link for our proprietary at home workouts. So we're going to get into that next, um, into the customizable fitness portion of it, but this just says, Hey, we're going to send you the link for your workouts. Um, next it says after each workout, you complete market on your weekly tracker by writing, uh, on a scale of one to 10, how exhausted you are, um, after finishing, we'll get into the weekly trackers and I'll show you an example of the weekly tracker on this one here. Um, and exactly what it's used for, but this is just a reminder for them to write their workout on a scale of one to 10. And the idea behind that is as they get more and more fit. If they start writing, you know, they start off with nines and tens, but those same workouts in two months are sixes and sevens. Well, then you know, it's time to progress, time to make it harder. Right. And this is mostly for online clients, um, but you can still use this for HTTP clients and everything like that as well on their weekly tracker as they're sending it into you. So the weekly tracker is something they take a picture of when they're done with the week and then they send it to you. Um, next we're moving on to the recovery plan. So the recovery plan goes over three things, sleep, water and supplementation. So sleep, it's recommended that they sleep eight hours, um, uh, to recover fully. And so, uh, this is something else they're going to be checking off on their weekly tracker water. We recommend that they're drinking three liters or a hundred ounces of water each day, um, with a little tip of saying, Hey, drink a two cups of water before you're, uh, before your meals, it gets you halfway there. Um, and so they can check this off on their weekly tracker every single week as well. Then we go into supplementation and here, uh, it says supplements, containx your results. Uh, we recommend, and here's where you circle the, uh, supplements that you recommend for them. And this is where you would incorporate selling supplements into that half hour meeting and say like, these are the ones that you're going to need. Um, what flavors do you want type of deal? And you'll cross off the ones that they don't need from here. Be like, Hey, uh, you don't need this one. You don't need this one. You don't need this one. Um, and this is where you're actually circling and recommending the supplements that they take. Right. So that, uh, that covers the recovery portion of this. Um, then we're moving into accountability. So the customized accountability is going to be this weekly tracker. Um, plus, uh, a couple of other things that we'll get into in the accountability video, um, but it says, we'll be reaching out to you throughout the week to make sure everything is going according to plan. In addition to that, we'd like you to fill out this weekly tracker and send it to us, um, each week when it is complete. Here's an example. So we attach an example for them to see. So that way they know exactly how they should be filling it out. So, um, the top says eight hours of sleep last night, uh, tracked and, and ate all portions of food, um, no cheap meals or bad snacks, no alcohol, uh, worked out, sent weekly weigh in and drank 80 to a hundred ounces of water. So looking at that, um, sleeping eight hours, we already talked about that in recovery, they should be xing that off every night, um, if, if they can track and ate, uh, all portions, they should be xing that off. No cheap meals. We, we don't have that in the initial six week challenge or 42 day or, or whatever. So they should be xing off. No cheap meals, no alcohol. It's not part of the plan worked out. They should be, uh, xing the days that you discussed that they worked out. Um, that's that we just went over those days. They should be putting the next there with how difficult the workout was eight out of 10, nine out of 10, 10 out of 10, whatever. Um, sent weekly weigh in. So they should be sending you their, their weekly weigh in. So, um, just shooting a picture of it to you or writing it on the clipboard if they're at the gym, um, otherwise they should be sending you a picture of them on a scale or just at least what their weight is. So you can help them, uh, stay accountable to it. And then, uh, the hundred ounces, like we talked about in recovery, they should be marking that off and they have Sunday through Saturday. Right. So this is pretty self-explanatory, but this is them filling it out and, and, and you want to make sure that it's, it's, uh, that it is, uh, established as to when you want to receive this. So they're not sending it to you late. So they're not sending it to you incomplete on here. It says place an X in each box that you have successfully completed. Uh, this is due to your coach every Monday, text the pick, right? So, um, when they finish a Sunday night, first thing in the morning on Monday, they should be sending this to you and then starting a brand new one. So you'll see that it says week one. Uh, we do give you week one through six for them to fill out. Okay. So this is going to be the last part of the blueprint where we cover accountability. And then in the next videos, we're going to be touching on each one of these things specifically. So now we will jump into that final piece.
